title: Lastrea villosa
intro: Lastrea villosa, also known as the hairy fern, is a species of fern native to tropical Asia and parts of Africa. The hairy fern has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea villosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Hairy fern is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other medicinal plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
